replication_study.hypothesis: Among participants, the benefit-minus-symbolism difference score (ScoreDiff) will exhibit a significant interaction between Level (Category vs. Brand) and ProductType (Utilitarian vs. Symbolic). Specifically, relative to the Category level, branding will decrease ScoreDiff for utilitarian products (becoming relatively more symbolic) and increase ScoreDiff for symbolic products (becoming relatively more utilitarian).

data_plan.source_type: Online survey experiment (Qualtrics-style dataset with randomized conditions and attention checks).
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single wave; eight target products (four utilitarian, four symbolic), Category vs Brand conditions with two brand sets.
data_plan.sample_size: Raw N=637 rows in LeBeouf_replication_data.csv; final eligible N will be determined after pre-registered exclusions (Status==0, complete screenQ, Attention1==7, Attention2==1, screenQ==2).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participant aggregated by ProductType (within-subject) and Level/Condition (between-subject).
data_plan.access_details: Data and code are provided in the replication_data folder and must be accessible inside the container at /app/data.

qualification.explanation: The dataset directly operationalizes the same constructs and design as the original Study 1b: participants evaluate eight products spanning utilitarian and symbolic categories, at category vs brand levels, and provide benefit and symbol ratings. It enables construction of the difference score (Benefits − Symbols) and the 2×2 mixed ANOVA focal test. It is a new, independent sample collected under similar conditions, providing a valid test of robustness.
qualification.similarity_to_original: The original focal claim (Study 1b) tested a Level (Category vs Brand) × Product Type (Utilitarian vs Symbolic) interaction on benefit–symbolism difference scores, F(1, 204) = 7.70, p = .006 (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json ‘Study 1b’ entry; original_paper.pdf pp. 351–352, Table 2). The replication dataset contains the same eight product categories and the same operationalization via benefit and symbolism ratings with ItemCatID and ProductType labels (ItemsList_Final.csv head shows Paper Towels, College T-shirts, American Flags, Toothpaste, Class Rings; data/original/23/input/replication_data/ItemsList_Final.csv). The R code constructs ScoreDiff = Benefits − Symbols and runs the identical 2×2 mixed ANOVA (LeBoeuf_JournMarketRes_2010_EKBZ_Yoon_Direct Replication_618K_Final.R).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original data used in-lab undergraduate samples with specific brands and conditions (post_registration.json: Study 1b N=206; original_paper.pdf pp. 351–352). The replication uses a new online sample (Qualtrics-style; LeBeouf_replication_data.csv collected in 2020 per timestamps) with attention checks and screening (Attention1, Attention2, screenQ), and may employ two brand name sets (Cond mapping) as per ItemsList_Final.csv. While products and operationalization match, the sample, time period, and data collection mode differ, providing an independent test (replication_data folder; LeBeouf_replication_data.csv; ItemsList_Final.csv).

data_plan.notes: Columns in LeBeouf_replication_data.csv include replicated item ratings with Qualtrics-derived names (e.g., '1_Util...11', '1_Symbol...12', etc.). The analysis drops Familiarity and Bipolar items and aggregates over items within each ProductType. Condition coding: Cond==1 or 2 are Category (NoBrand); Cond==3 or 4 are Brand (two brand sets). Paths must be /app/data inside the container. The R scripts were updated to read ItemsList_Final.csv (not .xlsx) and to setwd('/app/data').

steps: Apply pre-registered exclusions: Status==0; screenQ not missing and equals 2; Attention1==7; Attention2==1.
Remove administrative and non-DV columns (StartDate:Q1, Familiarity*, Bipol*). Create participant ID (ID).
Split dataset into Category (Cond==1 or 2), BrandA (Cond==3), BrandB (Cond==4) subsets; drop columns not relevant to each subset’s item set.
Reshape each subset from wide to long using ItemCatID, creating Benefits and Symbols columns.
Add Condition factor: Category vs Brand; bind subsets and join with ItemsList_Final.csv by ItemCatID and Cond to attach ProductType and Category labels.
Collapse to participant-level means by ProductType and Condition.
Run 2×2 mixed ANOVA: ScoreDiff ~ Condition*ProductType + Error(ID/ProductType).
Report the Level × ProductType interaction F-test, partial eta-squared, and simple contrasts (emmeans) within ProductType and within Condition.
Optionally visualize group means with SE by ProductType and Condition.
planned_method.models: Mixed ANOVA: ScoreDiff ~ Condition (between) * ProductType (within) with Error(ID/ProductType). Post-hoc pairwise comparisons via emmeans; effect size via partial eta-squared.
planned_method.outcome_variable: ScoreDiff = mean(Benefits − Symbols) per participant × ProductType × Condition (collapsed across relevant items).
planned_method.independent_variables: Condition (Category vs Brand; between-subject), ProductType (Utilitarian vs Symbolic; within-subject).
planned_method.control_variables: Design controls inherent to the dataset: counterbalanced product orders, brand set assignment (Cond 3 vs 4) merged into Brand level; analysis aggregates across item identities within ProductType.
planned_method.tools_software: R (>=4.1) with packages: readr, readxl (not required if using CSV mapping), tidyverse, ggplot2, kableExtra, pander, dplyr, emmeans, sjstats, tibble, reshape2, ggpubr.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: The Level × ProductType interaction effect on ScoreDiff (partial eta-squared and F-statistic).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: F-test from the mixed ANOVA for the interaction term; follow-up t-tests (emmeans) within ProductType and within Condition for direction checks.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion by applying exclusion filters and dropping incomplete cases implicit in reshaping. No imputation.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Primary inference is the single focal interaction; if reporting post-hoc contrasts, use Tukey-adjusted p-values as in the provided code for confirmatory checks.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-sided tests with alpha=0.05. Successful replication requires (a) a significant Level × ProductType interaction with (b) the predicted direction: branding reduces ScoreDiff for utilitarian products and increases ScoreDiff for symbolic products.

files.LeBoeuf_JournMarketRes_2010_EKBZ_Yoon_Di: Main analysis script. Loads data from /app/data, applies exclusions, reshapes to long, merges item metadata, computes ScoreDiff, collapses by participant × ProductType × Condition, and runs the 2×2 mixed ANOVA with post-hoc contrasts and effect sizes. Updated to use /app/data and ItemsList_Final.csv.
R Markdown report version of the main analysis pipeline with the same data steps and ANOVA, plus a bar plot. Updated to use /app/data and ItemsList_Final.csv.
Earlier/alternate script referencing different file names/paths (data.csv, ItemsList.xlsx). Not used for this run; keep for reference only.
files.LeBeouf_replication_data.csv: Raw participant-level dataset containing item ratings (Benefits/Symbols for eight products), attention checks, condition indicators (Cond), and metadata.
files.ItemsList_Final.csv: Item mapping table with ItemCatID, Category, ProductType (Utilitarian/Symbolic), Brand/BrandType, and Cond, enabling merge and labeling.

codebase.notes: All data reads must occur from /app/data. Scripts have been minimally edited to setwd('/app/data') and to read the CSV mapping (ItemsList_Final.csv). No outputs are currently written to disk; console output includes ANOVA tables and contrasts. Ensure required R packages are installed. If rendering Rmd, rmarkdown is needed. Qualtrics-style duplicated column names are handled by selective column drops and wide→long reshape as coded.

analysis.instructions:
- Place LeBoeuf_JournMarketRes_2010_EKBZ_Yoon_Direct Replication_618K_Final.R (or the Rmd) and the two CSV files into a runtime environment with /app/data mounted to ./data.
- Ensure required R packages are installed (see docker_specs).
- Run: Rscript 'data/original/23/input/replication_data/LeBoeuf_JournMarketRes_2010_EKBZ_Yoon_Direct Replication_618K_Final.R' (the script will setwd('/app/data') and read LeBeouf_replication_data.csv and ItemsList_Final.csv).
- Inspect console output for ANOVA summary of ScoreDiff ~ Condition*ProductType + Error(ID/ProductType), partial eta-squared, and emmeans contrasts.
- Optional: Render the Rmd to HTML: R -e "rmarkdown::render('data/original/23/input/replication_data/LeBoeuf_JournMarketRes_2010_EKBZ_Yoon_Direct Replication_618K_Final.Rmd')".
analysis.comparison_metrics:
- Statistical significance and direction of the Level × ProductType interaction (target: significant, p<.05).
- Directional checks via simple contrasts: Branding decreases ScoreDiff for utilitarian products and increases ScoreDiff for symbolic products.
- Effect size comparison: partial eta-squared for the interaction vs. original’s qualitative benchmark; compare pattern of means to Table 2 in original_paper.pdf.
- Report group means by Condition × ProductType for transparency.
